input.prompt: Your team is upgrading the codebase from .NET 9 to .NET 10. You have been asked to benchmark AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount to check how the upgrade will impact their performance. The source code is in the TextAnalyzer subdirectory (TextAnalyzer.cs and TextAnalyzer.csproj). We don't need anything too comprehensive, don't spend more than 10 minutes on this. Don't worry about cleaning up any files or projects you create along the way. Note: this machine may be under load from other processes. If the .NET 9 runtime is missing, install it using the official dotnet-install script from https://dot.net/v1/dotnet-install.sh (or dotnet-install.ps1 on Windows).

data.content: Both .NET 9 and 10 runtimes are available. I'll create a BenchmarkDotNet project that multi-targets net9.0 and net10.0 to compare performance.
data.reasoningText:
Now I need to create a BenchmarkDotNet project that benchmarks AverageWordLength, WordLengthHistogram, and UniqueWordCount across both net9.0 and net10.0. I'll create a benchmark project that multi-targets both frameworks so BenchmarkDotNet can compare them directly.

Let me create the benchmark project.

toolCalls.name: create

toolResult.sessionLog:
diff --git a/tmp/sv-e21ba64f23db4846929b0701c0785e79/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks.csproj b/tmp/sv-e21ba64f23db4846929b0701c0785e79/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks.csproj
create file mode 100644
index 0000000..0000000
--- a/dev/null
+++ b/tmp/sv-e21ba64f23db4846929b0701c0785e79/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks/TextAnalyzer.Benchmarks.csproj
@@ -1,0 +1,15 @@
+<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
+  <PropertyGroup>
+    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
+    <TargetFrameworks>net9.0;net10.0</TargetFrameworks>
+    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
+    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
+  </PropertyGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <PackageReference Include="BenchmarkDotNet" Version="0.14.0" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+  <ItemGroup>
+    <Compile Include="../TextAnalyzer/TextAnalyzer.cs" Link="TextAnalyzer.cs" />
+  </ItemGroup>
+</Project>
+

data.content: BDN 0.14.0 doesn't have the Net10 moniker yet. I'll switch to using the `--runtimes` CLI flag instead.
data.reasoningText:
BenchmarkDotNet 0.14.0 doesn't have Net100 yet. Let me check what monikers are available, or I could use a newer version of BenchmarkDotNet, or use a custom job configuration.

Let me try using `BenchmarkDotNet.Environments.CoreRuntime.Core100` or configure jobs manually. Actually, let me check what's available in BDN 0.14.0 for .NET 10.

Actually the simplest approach: BDN 0.14.0 might not support .NET 10 yet. Let me try the nightly/latest version. Or I can use a custom job with a specific runtime path.


I could skip the runtime specification entirely and let multi-targeting handle it naturally, but that requires setting up jobs anyway. The CLI approach with `--runtimes` might be cleaner, or I could manually configure a job with a custom CoreRuntime.

Configuring job with custom runtime...
